name: Cima del Montgó por la ruta gris — circular desde el Campo de tiro de Dénia
region_intro: Family friendly hiking trails in El Montgó are set within a majestic limestone massif that rises sharply from sea level between Dénia and Jávea. The region is characterized by dramatic cliffs, Mediterranean scrubland, and regenerating pine forests, offering diverse landscapes for exploration. Its proximity to the Mediterranean Sea provides extensive coastal views and unique contrasts in the terrain. The geological composition has also led to fascinating rock formations and sea cliffs, particularly around Cap de Sant Antoni. Best family friendly hiking trails in El Montgó The most popular family friendly hiking route is Cape San Antonio Windmills Loop, a 2.7 miles (4.4 km) trail that takes 1 hour 9 minutes to complete. This easy route explores historical windmills and offers coastal views. Another top favourite among local hikers is El Faro Cabo de San Antonio — circular by Jávea, a moderate 2.7 miles (4.4 km) path. This trail provides views of the lighthouse and the coastline near Jávea. Local hikers also love the Plana de Sant Jeroni loop from Reserva Natural dels Fons Marins del Cap de Sant Antoni, a 3.8 miles (6.2 km) trail leading through a natural reserve, often completed in about 1 hour 39 minutes. Hiking in El Montgó is defined by limestone cliffs, Mediterranean scrubland, and pine forests, with coastal paths offering panoramic sea views. The network offers options for easy family walks as well as more challenging ascents. The routes in El Montgó are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 4,600 reviews. More than 24,000 hikers have used komoot to explore El Montgó's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many family-friendly hiking trails are available in El Montgó?
A: El Montgó offers a wide selection of family-friendly hiking trails. You'll find over 25 routes specifically curated for families, with 15 of them being easy and 13 of moderate difficulty, ensuring there's something for every age and ability.

Q: What kind of terrain can we expect on family hikes in El Montgó?
A: The family-friendly trails in El Montgó feature diverse terrain, from gentle paths through aromatic pine forests and Mediterranean scrubland to coastal routes with stunning sea views. While some sections might be rocky, many easy routes are well-maintained and suitable for children. The region's limestone geology also means you might encounter fascinating rock formations.

Q: Are there any circular family-friendly trails in El Montgó?
A: Yes, there are several excellent circular routes perfect for families. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1222917773/cape-san-antonio-windmills-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cape San Antonio Windmills Loop&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 4.4 km trail that takes about an hour, offering historical insights and lovely views. Another great option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38234033" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plana de Sant Jeroni loop&lt;/a&gt;, which is 6.2 km and also considered easy.

Q: What interesting landmarks or natural features can families explore on hikes in El Montgó?
A: El Montgó is rich in attractions. Families can discover historical sites like the ancient &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/416894" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Torre del Gerro&lt;/a&gt; watchtower or the fascinating &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/68080" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cova Tallada (Cut Cave)&lt;/a&gt;, a sea-level cave. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/203776" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Windmills of Jávea&lt;/a&gt; also offer a glimpse into the region's past. Many trails provide breathtaking coastal views, especially around Cap de Sant Antoni.

Q: When is the best time of year for family hiking in El Montgó?
A: The Mediterranean climate of El Montgó makes spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November) ideal for family hiking. During these seasons, temperatures are mild, and the natural park is vibrant with flora. Summer can be very hot, so if you hike then, it's best to start early in the morning or late in the afternoon and choose shaded routes.

Q: Are there shaded family hiking paths in El Montgó?
A: While much of El Montgó features open Mediterranean scrubland, some family-friendly trails do pass through regenerating pine forests, offering welcome shade, especially during warmer months. It's always advisable to check the specific route details and plan accordingly, bringing hats and plenty of water.

Q: Can I bring my dog on family hikes in El Montgó?
A: El Montgó Natural Park is generally dog-friendly, but it's important to keep your dog on a leash to protect the diverse wildlife and flora, including species like the endangered Bonelli's eagle. Always ensure you carry enough water for your pet and clean up after them.

Q: Are there trails suitable for toddlers or very young children?
A: For toddlers and very young children, look for the 'easy' rated trails with minimal elevation gain and shorter distances. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1222917773/cape-san-antonio-windmills-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cape San Antonio Windmills Loop&lt;/a&gt; are relatively flat and well-suited for little legs or strollers designed for light off-road use. Always consider the duration and terrain when planning with very young children.

Q: Is parking available near the family-friendly trailheads in El Montgó?
A: Yes, parking is generally available near popular trailheads and access points for family walks in El Montgó, particularly around areas like Cap de Sant Antoni and the starting points in Dénia or Jávea. It's always a good idea to arrive early during peak season to secure a spot.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about family hiking in El Montgó?
A: The komoot community highly rates hiking in El Montgó, with an average score of 4.4 stars. Reviewers often praise the stunning panoramic views of the Mediterranean Sea, the diverse landscapes, and the opportunity to combine nature exploration with visits to historical sites like the Torre del Gerro. The well-marked paths and variety of easy routes also make it a favorite for families.

Q: Are there any family-friendly routes that lead to caves?
A: Absolutely! The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33694397" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cova Tallada – Torre del Gerro loop from Xàbia / Jávea&lt;/a&gt;, while rated difficult due to its length, offers access to the famous &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/68080" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cova Tallada (Cut Cave)&lt;/a&gt;. For a less strenuous option, you can often find shorter, more direct paths to explore caves like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4039997" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cuva del Agua&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1088111" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cova de l'Aigua (Eye of Montgó)&lt;/a&gt;, which are fascinating for children.
tips: The top of the Montgó offers unique and amazing views of the coast of Denia and Jávea. You get there along a path that in sections can be somewhat demanding due to the slopes but that is very enjoyable at every step. I recommend going around it and returning along the Camí de Colonia del Montogó, which runs along its entire length facing the sea, a wonderful walk!
The little detour down is worth it. Recommended lamp.
This is a very do-able hike. Not too heavy. There is a good parking near Club de Tiro Javea. To reach the top of the Montgo is quite a challenge but also very possible. Enjoy the views!

waypoints.name: View of Dénia and Xàbia from Montgó
